evidence_summary: Infratil reports CDC's FY34 built-capacity pipeline at 2906MW across Australia and New Zealand: 671MW operating, 572MW under construction, and 1663MW future build. Australia-only capacity is approximately 2682MW after excluding Auckland disclosed capacity, subject to rounding in the regional table. Current named CDC rows in the project database sum to 1789MW.
treatment_notes: Track aggregate guidance separately. Do not add the unmatched Australian future-build MW to project totals until named site-level evidence exists.

announcement_name: CDC signs 555MW data centre contract with US customer
evidence_summary: CDC secured a 555MW 30-year contract with a US customer and states total contracted capacity exceeds 1GW. The contracted capacity will be delivered across CDC campuses already under development over FY28 and FY29.
treatment_notes: Demand and contract overlay only. The contract is within CDC's current growth plan and should not be added as extra project MW without campus allocation.

announcement_name: Record Contracted Utilisation and A$2.2bn Capital Plan
source: NEXTDC ASX announcement and investor presentation
evidence_summary: NEXTDC reports contracted utilisation rising to 667MW, forward order book expanding to 544MW, and a record 250MW customer commitment at S4 Sydney. The announcement also describes a funded A$2.2bn capital plan.
treatment_notes: Track as operator-level contracted demand and funding signal. Do not add contracted utilisation to physical project MW because the project table already stores site-level planned capacity where disclosed.

geography: Global portfolio with Sydney project line
evidence_summary: Goodman reports a 5.0GW global data-centre power bank, with 3.4GW secured power, 0.3GW work in progress, and 0.8GW delivered. Its active-sites table includes SYD01 Sydney at 90MW total by June 2026.
treatment_notes: Track as aggregate developer guidance and as a project-lead candidate. Keep out of default AU project MW until SYD01 has row-level source treatment in the project database.

source: Macquarie Technology Group Investor Day presentation

evidence_summary: AirTrunk states its Australian platform will span five campuses: SYD1 121MW+, SYD2 158MW+, SYD3 330MW+, MEL1 276MW+, and MEL2 354MW+, delivering more than 1.2GW combined. The current project table already stores those site-level campus rows.
